commit.message:
Initial plan (#21)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge Phase 8 v2 PRs + EngCode + Repo Restructure; fix test_all (#20)

* Phase 8.1: Renderer Backend Abstraction — vtable + framebuffer + SDL2 stub

Extends Tervas (Phase 7) with pluggable renderer backends:
- TvRendererVTable: init/destroy/render_frame/render_to_buffer/get_caps
- ASCII vtable (existing renderer wrapped)
- Framebuffer vtable (TvRenderCell → GuiPixel via 8-bit palette)
- SDL2 stub vtable (CPU fallback, Phase 9+ for real SDL2)
- TvFrame: stack → heap allocation (supports 256×256 = 65536 cells)
- TV_FRAME_ASCII_CELLS for backward compat
- All conversions integer-only (R-6, DK-2)

4 new tests (P8F-T1~T4), all existing 160+ tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Phase 8.2: Unified Event Bus + Mouse Adapter

Central ring-buffer event queue (256 capacity) unifying keyboard,
mouse, timer, and system events. Overflow drops oldest, keeps newest.

- EventBus: push/poll/peek_type/clear API
- MouseState: screen→canvas coordinate transform (integer-only, DK-2)
- CanvasEvent tagged union (11 event types)
- WH-compatible type mapping for deterministic replay

5 new tests (P8F-T5~T9), all existing tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Phase 8.4: canvas:// URI Scheme — parser + serializer

Implements canvas://lane.tile/path?tick=N URI format:
- URI parser: canvas://, local path (/), error handling
- Integer-only parsing (DK-2, no strtoul/strtof)
- Reverse serialization (URI → string roundtrip)
- lane_id 0-255, tile_id 0-65535, path max 256 chars
- tick parameter for historical resource access

5 new tests (P8F-T15~T19), all existing tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Phase 8.3: Dynamic Scheduler v2 — heap pool + priority + zombie TTL

Extends ProcTable (PROC8_MAX=256 static) with dynamic scheduling:
- Heap-allocated pool: configurable up to 1024 processes
- 4 priority levels: REALTIME (every tick), NORMAL (1/2), BACKGROUND (1/4), IDLE (1/8)
- Process groups: group_id for batch operations
- Auto zombie reaping: configurable TTL (default 64 ticks)
- run_frame(): execute N ticks per frame call
- Backward compatible: sched_v2_tick() alone works like proc_tick()

5 new tests (P8F-T10~T14), all existing tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add BH lifecycle summary to zombie reaping

When a zombie process is reaped after TTL, the scheduler now analyzes
its lifetime [born_tick, zombie_tick] via bh_analyze_window() and
records the pattern (IDLE/LOOP/BURST) as a BH_SUMMARY in WhiteHole.

This enables:
- Process forensics: why did this process die?
- Energy prediction: BH patterns inform future spawn energy budgets
- Determinism: BH records are WH-compatible, fully replayable

Safe when ctx=NULL (BH step skipped). Reap stats tracked via
sched_v2_get_reap_stats().

New test: P8F-T14b (BH reap integration). 6/6 PASS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Phase 8.5: Compositor — 16-layer composition with dirty rect optimization

Composites up to 16 layers bottom-to-top with integer alpha blending:
- pixel_blend(): (src*a + dst*(256-a)) >> 8, with 255×255 precision fix
- Dirty rect tracking: only recompose changed regions
- Layer properties: visible, alpha, offset, name
- compositor_compose(): full redraw or incremental dirty-only path
- Game engine: background → tiles → sprites → HUD → overlay
- Browser: page → fixed-header → popup → devtools

Depends on Phase 8.1 (GuiBuffer from renderer backend).

5 new tests (P8F-T20~T24), all existing tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor 8.2: Event Bus → WH-Backed Pending Queue

BREAKING CHANGE: Events now require commit() before poll().

Architecture shift (Canvas OS principles enforced):
- [원칙1] Ring buffer is a pending cache, not source of truth
- [원칙2] event_bus_commit() records ALL events to WhiteHole via
  wh_push_io_event() — deterministic replay now possible
- [원칙3] DK-1 tick boundary: uncommitted events cannot be polled.
  Sequence: push() → commit(apply_tick) → poll()
- DEV_TIMER added to WhDevice for timer event WH recording

API change:
- event_bus_bind_ctx(bus, ctx) — attach EngineContext for WH
- event_bus_commit(bus, apply_tick) — flush pending → WH + committed
- event_bus_poll() — only returns committed events
- ctx=NULL safe path for unit testing (commit logic runs, WH skipped)

5 tests updated (P8F-T5~T9), all existing 160+ tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor 8.6: Frame Loop → Tick-Based Deterministic (no wall-clock)

BREAKING CHANGE: wall-clock removed. EngineContext.tick is the only time.

Architecture shift (Canvas OS principles enforced):
- [원칙1] total_ticks = deterministic time counter (not wall-clock)
- [원칙2] Timer via DEV_TIMER WH records (event_bus_commit handles it)
- [원칙3] DK-1 tick boundary: commit() → input() → tick() → render()
  Events are committed to WH BEFORE simulation runs.
- [원칙4] No clock_gettime — replay produces identical results
- FrameTimer struct removed (was wall-clock dependent)

Determinism proof: same ticks_per_frame + same events → same total_ticks
regardless of when or how fast the loop runs.

5 tests updated (P8F-T25~T29), all existing 160+ tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor 8.3: Scheduler → Canvas-Resident Process Meta Cells

BREAKING CHANGE: process state now lives in canvas cells (Y=960).

Architecture shift (Canvas OS principles enforced):
- [원칙1] Canvas-as-Memory: process meta cell is source of truth
  cell.A=pid, cell.B=state, cell.G=energy(DK-4 clamped), cell.R=priority|group
- Proc8Ext is a cache/view — sync_to_canvas/sync_from_canvas
- spawn: writes meta cell → cache sync
- tick: reads/decrements energy → writes back to canvas cell
- exit: writes ZOMBIE to cell.B
- reap: clears cell (A=B=G=R=0) → frees slot
- BH lifecycle summary preserved from v1

ctx=NULL safe path: canvas sync skipped (unit test compatible).

New test: P8F-T14c (canvas cell sync verification). 7/7 PASS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor 8.4: URI → Gate-Aware Canvas Cell Resolution

Architecture shift (Canvas OS principles enforced):
- [원칙1] canvas_uri_open() resolves URI → canvas tile cell offset
- [원칙4] Gate check: gate closed → URI_ERR_GATE_CLOSED
- canvas_uri_read_cell() reads canvas cells through handle
- tile_id → flat cell index calculation (32×32 tiles)
- Local paths bypass gate (always accessible)
- ctx=NULL → URI_ERR_NO_CTX

New test: P8F-T19b (gate-aware open + cell read). 6/6 PASS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor 8.5: Compositor → Gate-Controlled Layers

Architecture shift (Canvas OS principles enforced):
- [원칙1] Each layer has tile_id linking to canvas tile
- [원칙4] Layer visibility = gate_is_open(tile_id)
  No more external bool visible flag — gate state is truth
- compositor_layer_visible() queries gate via EngineContext
- Gate close → layer invisible. Gate open → visible.
- ctx=NULL: all layers default visible (test compatible)
- Removed compositor_set_visible() — gate controls this now

5 tests updated (P8F-T20~T24) including gate visibility test.
All existing 160+ tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor 8.1: Renderer → Display Tile Commit to Canvas

Architecture shift (Canvas OS principles enforced):
- [원칙1] tv_commit_to_display() writes GuiBuffer pixels to canvas cells
  Display area: Y=0..31, X=0..63 (canvas top-left)
  cell.R=red, cell.G=green, cell.B=blue, cell.A=flat_index
- Rendering result now lives IN the canvas (not just external buffer)
- External systems can read display by inspecting canvas cells directly

New test: P8F-T4b (display tile commit). 5/5 PASS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add EngCode — 1-letter English opcode shorthand (articulatory order)

New input system: single English letter = opcode category.
Shift-minimized delimiters: number-attach, : ' " ` - , ~

Letter map (by place of articulation):
  Bilabial:    P(Print) B(Block) M(Move) W(Write)
  Alveolar:    T(Tick) D(Draw) N(Number) L(Load) S(Save) Z(Zero) R(Replay)
  Palatal:     J(Jump) Y(Yank)
  Velar:       G(Gate) K(Kill) C(Call)
  Glottal:     H(Halt) X(Exec)
  Special:     A(Address) E(Energy) V(Value) I(Info) Q(Quit) F(Find) O(Open) U(Undo)

Examples:
  G10 E:64 V'A' W    (gate open 10, energy 64, value 'A', write)

Same delimiters as EngCode: number-attach, : ' " ` - , ~

* Rewrite HgCode v2 — keyboard matrix (consonant × modifier = opcode)

BREAKING: Complete redesign from single-letter to matrix system.

Left hand (6 category keys):
  ㅅ(수/Arithmetic) ㄷ(뎃/Data) ㅎ(화/IO) ㄱ(기/System) ㅂ(분/Branch) ㄹ(론/Logic)

Right hand (8 modifier keys):
  ㅏ(+) ㅓ(-) ㅗ(↑) ㅜ(↓) ?(cond) !(force) #(def) .(end)

6×8 = 48 opcodes, zero memorization needed.
Examples:
  ㅅㅏ=ADD  ㅅㅓ=SUB  ㅎ#=GATE_ON  ㅎ.=GATE_OFF
  ㄱㅓ=HALT ㅂㅏ=JMP  ㄹ#=LOOP     ㄹ.=END

Direction (WASD layout): ㅈ(↑) ㅁ(←) ㄴ(↓) ㅇ(→)
Utility: ㅋ(commit) ㅌ(tick) ㅍ(map)

Modifier consistency rule:
  ㅏ(+) always = base action (ADD, LOAD, PRINT, NOP, JMP, AND)
  .(end) always = terminate (INC, FREE, GATE_OFF, RESET, ENDDEF, END)

commit.message:
Add transaction history blocks to NFC card: anti-tamper checksum, atomic writes, last-4 display (#23)

* Initial plan

* Implement transaction history data blocks for NFC card

- Add TransactionBlock.kt: new data class with 32-byte layout (2 blocks)
  spanning initTimestamp (4B), up to 4 tx entries (6B each: 3B seconds
  offset, 1B op, 2B amount), and 4-byte HMAC-SHA256 checksum keyed with
  PSK+UID for anti-tamper detection.
- Update MainActivity to read/write all 3 data blocks; verify checksum
  before every add/deduct (refuse with error on tamper); retry interrupted
  writes using in-memory PendingWrite state; initialize transaction blocks
  with timestamp on format; display last 4 transactions after card read.
- Add transaction history section to activity_main.xml (gone by default).
- Add new string resources for transaction display and error messages.

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/f5b9270f-88fe-4b32-9a2e-f25468f92a31

* Convert balance counter to Mifare Classic Value Block format

- Add Byte.inv8() helper (xor 0xFF) since Kotlin's Byte.inv() is Int
- Add makeValueBlock(value): builds 16-byte Mifare Classic Value Block:
  value(4B LE) | ~value(4B) | value(4B) | addr ~addr addr ~addr
- Add readValueBlock(data): parses and validates triple-redundancy,
  returns null if block is not a valid Value Block
- readAndShowBalance: parse balance with readValueBlock()
- readAndDeduct: parse with readValueBlock(), build checksum with
  makeValueBlock(newBalance), use mifare.decrement()+transfer()
  (atomic chip-level operation) instead of writeBlock()
- addBalanceToCard: same — readValueBlock() + increment()+transfer()
- formatCard (both paths): initialise counter with makeValueBlock(0)
  instead of ByteArray(16) (all zeros)
- Recovery (PendingWrite): writeBlock() with full Value Block bytes
- Add MAX_SECONDS_OFFSET constant to TransactionBlock (24-bit limit)
- Update class-level KDoc to describe Value Block format

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/2d610a0f-f9cc-4144-8879-8d588663dead

* Show transactions always, use HH:mm:ss timestamps, remove monospace font

- readAndDeduct: call showTransactionHistory(txBlock) before early
  return on insufficient balance, so transaction history is always shown
- showTransactionHistory: replace +Ns relative-seconds label with
  wall-clock time (HH:mm:ss today, dd/MM HH:mm:ss on other days);
  add java.util.{Calendar,Date,SimpleDateFormat,Locale} imports
- strings.xml: remove tx_time_format (no longer used)
- activity_main.xml: remove android:fontFamily="monospace" from all
  four tvTx TextViews so they use the app's default sans-serif font

Co-authored-by: duhow <1145001+duhow@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/af6b3564-3c8e-4a7c-ada4-957468887738

* Flash red on invalid card read; show debug checksums at end of transaction list

- readAndShowBalance: add uid/psk extraction, call isValid() after parsing
  txBlock; flash red background and show card_tampered status when invalid
  (card is still read-only so balance and transactions are still shown)
- TransactionBlock: add extractChecksums() companion method returning
  Pair(storedChecksum, computedChecksum), reusing private constants
- MainActivity: add tvTxDebug TextView field + init; add showDebugChecksums()
  helper that populates tvTxDebug when debug mode is enabled; call
  showDebugChecksums after every showTransactionHistory invocation across
  readAndShowBalance, readAndDeduct, addBalanceToCard, and formatCard
- After successful deduct/add, debug checksums reflect the new (written)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/duhow/nfc-coins/sessions/d7549969-3131-4d45-ab41-def351780878

* Add "Verificar integridad de tarjeta" setting (enabled by default)

When disabled, invalid checksums in write operations (readAndDeduct,
addBalanceToCard) are ignored and the transaction proceeds normally.
The written blocks always get a fresh valid checksum via toBytes().
Interrupted-write retry logic is unaffected (runs before the setting check).
Read-only path (readAndShowBalance) still shows red background regardless.

Add DynCaller::set_default_coerce global flag

Adapter authors can now enable coercion process-wide at startup rather
than the user having to specify it in every descriptor string:

  DynCaller::set_default_coerce(true);

The global flag is OR-d with the per-descriptor coerce flag, so existing
descriptors that already include 'coerce' continue to work unchanged.
DynCaller::default_coerce() returns the current setting.

feat(Frame): auto-hide back button on mobile, wire system back gesture

On platforms with a native back gesture (Android, iOS), the visual back
button is hidden via OnFormFactor and navigation is handled by
subscribing to TopLevel.BackRequestedEvent. This follows the same
pattern as MasterDetailsNavigator.RequiresBackButton().

The system back handler executes BackCommand when available and marks the
event as handled; if no BackCommand is set, the event bubbles up to
allow the OS default behavior (e.g., closing the app).

Desktop platforms retain the visual back button as before.
